selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 5.9% and 20-day momentum is 11.4%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 11.2% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 3.5%, downside volatility near 2.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 9.6%.
Volume confirmation is -5.0% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.8% versus SPY over 5 days and 2.9% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.78, confidence 0.66, and 0.4 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.78, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 2 sources, 0.4 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.82, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.66.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.01).
Overall model score is 0.156 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Meta Is Betting $50 Billion on AI Data Centers. Where Does The Stock Go From Here?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Directly discusses Meta's large AI data-center spending (~$50B) and insider selling, and cites analyst price targets. This is the most directly relevant for short-term sentiment, but appears to be analytical commentary rather than reporting of a new, unexpected corporate action, so material near-term impact is limited-to-moderate.

news_evidence.title: What Makes Meta Platforms (META) a Compelling Long-Term Opportunity?
news_evidence.llm_reason: This is a long-term bull case derived from an investor letter. It reiterates positive fundamentals but provides no new company-specific catalyst likely to move the stock within the next 1-10 trading days. Low short-term relevance; mainly useful for long-horizon sentiment.
